label: USA / English
ladderNote: U.S. Gen Z / millennial dating vocabulary from r/dating and r/AskWomen. Hookup and dating tracks often skip levels.

levels.meaning: Mutual attraction or interest, nothing acted on yet.
Showing interest; banter, compliments, light pursuit.
Regular texting / chatting with romantic intent; usually not exclusive.

levels.term: Hanging out / getting to know each other
levels.meaning: Spending time together without a defined label.
Casual sex, often one-off or low-commitment.
Friendship plus recurring sex; usually no romantic label.
Going on dates; may still see other people.
Ongoing romantic/sexual gray zone without clear commitment.
Agreed not to date others; may still avoid boyfriend/girlfriend labels.
Defined romantic relationship with the label.
Serious couple language; often more adult than bf/gf.
First mutual "I love you" — a classic U.S. relationship milestone after things are official.
Unmarried or former co-parent in U.S. slang. The AAVE terms can be neutral in-group but may sound pejorative when applied by outsiders.
